seoTitle: Crop an Image Into a Circle in Google Docs
metaDescription: Turn a rectangular photo into a clean circular graphic in Google Docs using the Mask Image tool, without needing any external design software.
intro: You don't need Photoshop or Canva to get a circular profile-style photo into your document. Google Docs has a built-in masking tool that turns any rectangular image into a clean oval or circle. This guide walks through selecting the mask, adjusting it into a perfect circle, and styling it for a polished look.

steps.heading: Insert your image
steps.body: Upload or select the image you want to reshape directly inside your document.

steps.heading: Open the Mask Image tool
steps.body: Find the Mask Image dropdown next to the standard Crop icon on the image toolbar.

steps.heading: Choose the Oval shape
steps.body: Select the Oval frame from the shape grid to convert your image's outline into a rounded mask.

steps.heading: Adjust to a perfect circle
steps.body: Use the layout boundary handles to fine-tune a skewed oval into a symmetrical 1:1 circle.

steps.heading: Style borders and text wrap
steps.body: Apply a subtle border and line weight for a clean frame, then adjust text wrapping so paragraphs flow smoothly around the new circular graphic.

faq.question: Do I need a design tool to make a circular photo in Google Docs?
faq.answer: No, the built-in Mask Image tool handles it directly inside the document without needing external software.

faq.question: Why is my circle actually an oval?
faq.answer: This usually happens when the image's original aspect ratio isn't square; adjusting the boundary handles to a 1:1 ratio fixes it.

faq.question: Can I add a border around the circular image?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply custom border thickness and color accents after masking the image.

faq.question: Will text wrap properly around a circular image?
faq.answer: Yes, adjusting the text wrapping settings after masking keeps paragraphs flowing cleanly around the shape.
